WEAPON.NAME: Name
WEAPON_MODIFICATION.NAME: Name
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.ACCURATE: While benefiting from an Aim action, the weapon grants an extra +10 to Ballistic Skill on top of the normal Aim bonus. When firing a single shot from a single Basic weapon with this quality while Aiming, the attack deals an extra 1d10 damage for every two degrees of success beyond the first, up to a maximum of +2d10; these extra dice cannot trigger Righteous Fury.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.BALANCED: Grants a +10 bonus to Weapon Skill tests made to Parry. Wielding more than one Balanced weapon still only grants the bonus once.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.BLAST: On a hit, everyone within the blast radius in metres (the number in parentheses, X) also suffers one hit; roll damage once and apply it to each affected character. A Blast weapon scatters whenever the firer fails the Ballistic Skill test.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.CONCUSSIVE: A struck target must make a Toughness test at a penalty of 10 times the value in parentheses (X) — so Concussive (2) is -20, and Concussive (0) imposes no penalty. On a failure the target is Stunned for 1 round per degree of failure, and if the hit also dealt damage greater than the target's Strength bonus, the target is knocked Prone.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.CORROSIVE: On a hit, the Armour points of the armour worn on the struck location are reduced by 1d10. Any excess beyond the armour's points (or the full amount if no armour is worn there) is dealt to the target and is not reduced by Toughness. Armour reductions are cumulative and can be repaired with a Challenging (+0) Tech-Use test.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.CRIPPLING: A target that suffers at least one wound from this weapon is Crippled for the rest of the encounter or until fully healed. While Crippled, if the target takes more than a Half Action on his turn he suffers Rending damage equal to the value in parentheses (X) to the originally damaged Hit Location, ignoring Armour and Toughness.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.DEFENSIVE: Grants a +15 bonus on tests made to Parry with the weapon but imposes a -10 penalty on attacks made with it. (A shield is an exception: attacks with it instead take a -20 penalty.)
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.FELLING: When calculating the weapon's damage, reduce the target's Unnatural Toughness bonus by the value in parentheses (X). It only reduces Unnatural Toughness — never the base Toughness bonus — and the reduction applies only for that damage calculation.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.FLAME: Whenever a target is struck (even if it takes no damage), it must pass an Agility test or be set on fire. If the target is a vehicle, the pilot makes the appropriate Operate test with a bonus equal to the Armour value of the facing hit; failure sets the vehicle on fire.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.FLEXIBLE: The weapon cannot be Parried, but it can itself be used to Parry an opponent's weapon.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.FORCE: Counts as a Best-craftsmanship Mono version of the equivalent Low-Tech weapon. In a psyker's hands it gains bonus damage and penetration equal to his base psy rating and its damage becomes Energy. Whenever the psyker damages an opponent he may take a Half-Action Focus Power (Opposed Willpower); on success he deals +1d10 Energy per degree of success, ignoring Armour and Toughness bonus, always using base psy rating and never generating Psychic Phenomena on this test. Force weapons cannot be destroyed by the Power Field quality.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.GRAVITON: On a hit, inflicts additional damage equal to the target's Armour points on the struck location (or the Armour points of the struck facing if the target is a vehicle or cover). Vehicles that suffer Critical damage from Graviton weapons always roll on the Motive Systems Critical Effects table regardless of the location hit.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.HALLUCINOGENIC: A struck creature must pass a Toughness test at a penalty of 10 times the value in parentheses (X) or suffer a delusion, rolling 1d10 on the Hallucinogenic Effects table. Respirators and sealed armour grant +20 to this test. The effect lasts 1 round plus 1 additional round per degree of failure.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.HAYWIRE: Everything within the field's radius in metres (the value in parentheses, X) is affected — Haywire (3) is a 3-metre radius. Roll 1d10 on the Haywire Field Effects table (plus weapon modifiers) for the strength. The effect weakens one step in severity each round until it becomes Insignificant. Overlapping Haywire attacks do not stack; a new effect is ignored if weaker or replaces the old one if stronger.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.INACCURATE: The attacker gains no benefit from the Aim action when attacking with this weapon.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.INDIRECT: The weapon does not need line of sight, though the attacker must still be aware of the target's location; ranged attacks are made at -10 and require a Full Action instead of a Half Action. Each hit that lands deviates via the Scatter Diagram by 1d10 minus the firer's Ballistic Skill bonus metres (minimum 0), while each missed shot deviates by Xd10 metres, where X is the value in parentheses.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.LANCE: Penetration is variable based on accuracy: on a hit, increase the weapon's penetration by its base value once for each degree of success. For example, a base penetration of 5 hitting with three degrees of success adds 3x5=15, for a total penetration of 20.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.MAXIMAL: As a Free Action the weapon switches between Standard mode (normal profile) and Maximal. On Maximal it gains +10m range, +1d10 damage, and +2 penetration; any Blast value is increased by 2 (e.g. Blast [3] becomes Blast [5]). Firing on Maximal uses three times the normal ammunition per shot and gives the weapon the Recharge quality.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.MELTA: The weapon doubles its penetration when firing at Short range or closer.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.OVERHEATS: On an attack roll of 91 or higher the weapon overheats, dealing the wielder Energy damage equal to the weapon's damage at penetration 0 to an arm (the firing arm, or a random arm if fired two-handed); the wielder can avoid this by dropping the weapon as a Free Action. The weapon must spend the following round cooling and cannot fire again until the second round after overheating. It never jams — any effect that would cause a jam instead causes an overheat.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.POWER_FIELD: When this weapon successfully Parries an attack made by a weapon lacking this quality, roll 1d100; on 26 or higher the attacker's weapon is destroyed. Weapons with the Warp Weapon or Force quality and Natural Weapons are immune to this effect.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.PRIMITIVE: When rolling damage, any die result greater than the value in parentheses (X) counts as that number instead — Primitive (7) treats rolls of 8, 9, or 10 as 7. These weapons can still generate Righteous Fury normally on a damage roll of 10.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.PROVEN: When rolling damage, any die result lower than the value in parentheses (X) counts as that number instead — Proven (3) treats a roll of 1 or 2 as 3.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.RAZOR_SHARP: If the attack scores three or more degrees of success, the weapon's penetration value is doubled when resolving the hits from that attack.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.RECHARGE: After being used to attack, the weapon cannot attack again until the end of the next round — in effect it can only be fired once every other round.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.RELIABLE: The weapon only jams on an unmodified roll of 100. Reliable weapons with the Spray quality, or that make no hit rolls, never jam at all.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.SANCTIFIED: Damage dealt by this weapon counts as Holy damage, which can have special effects against certain Daemons and other Warp entities.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.SCATTER: At Point Blank range the weapon gains +10 to hit and +3 damage; at Short range it gains +10 to hit; at any longer range it suffers -3 damage.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.SHOCKING: A target that takes at least 1 point of damage (after Armour and Toughness bonus) must pass a Challenging (+0) Toughness test or suffer 1 level of Fatigue and be Stunned for a number of rounds equal to half its degrees of failure (rounded up).
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.SMOKE: Instead of dealing damage, a hit creates a smokescreen at the point of impact with a radius in metres equal to the value in parentheses (X). The screen lasts 1d10+10 rounds, or less in adverse weather.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.SNARE: A struck target must pass an Agility test at a penalty of 10 times the value in parentheses (X) or be Immobilised — Snare (1) imposes -10. An Immobilised target can only try to escape: as a Full Action it makes a Challenging (+0) Strength or Agility test at the same -10×X penalty to break free. The target is Helpless until it escapes.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.SPRAY: The weapon has a single range and hits everyone in a cone-shaped 30-degree arc out to its range with no Ballistic Skill test; each target must pass a Challenging (+0) Agility test or take one hit. Targets gain +20 to that test if the wielder lacks the appropriate Weapon Training (rising to +30 if the weapon is Heavy and unbraced). Cover only protects if it completely conceals the target. Hits always strike the Body, the weapon cannot make Called Shots, and it jams on a damage-die result of 9 (before modifiers).
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.STORM: Doubles the number of hits the weapon inflicts on the target and doubles the ammunition expended. For example, in fully automatic mode each degree of success yields two additional hits (up to the weapon's firing rate).
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.TEARING: The weapon rolls one extra die for damage and the lowest result is discarded.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.TOXIC: At the end of his turn, a character who suffered damage (after Armour and Toughness bonus) from this weapon during the last round must pass a Toughness test at a penalty of 10 times the value in parentheses (X) — Toxic (4) imposes -40 — or take 1d10 additional damage of the same type. Some weapons carry extra effects beyond this.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.TWIN_LINKED: Gains +20 to hit and uses twice as much ammunition when fired, and scores one additional hit if the attack succeeds by two or more degrees of success. When emptied, the weapon's reload time is doubled.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.UNBALANCED: The weapon cannot be used to make Lightning Attack actions and imposes a -10 penalty when used to Parry.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.UNRELIABLE: The weapon jams on a roll of 91 or higher, even when fired on Semi- or Full Auto.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.UNWIELDY: The weapon cannot be used to Parry or to make Lightning Attack actions.
WEAPON_QUALITY_DESC.VENGEFUL: The attacker triggers Righteous Fury on any damage-die result equal to or higher than the value in parentheses (X), before any modifiers are applied.
